theme: correlated fleet scheduling under hidden low-rank covariance
title: Correlated Fleet Scheduling: Partition for Expected Makespan
strategies: round-robin by index: send job i to machine i%k, ignoring both sizes and covariance (reproduces the evaluator's weak reference, ~0.1)

LPT on estimated means: sort jobs by probe-estimated mean processing time and drop each onto the least-mean-loaded machine -- balances first-order load but is blind to covariance, so it splits anti-correlated pairs

covariance-aware refine: seed with LPT then hill-climb the EMPIRICAL expected makespan over the probe scenarios via single-job relocations and pairwise swaps, discovering that co-locating anti-correlated jobs cancels their peaks
seeded metaheuristics on the empirical expected-makespan objective (perturb-and-reoptimize, tabu, simulated annealing) to squeeze the covariance structure further
